skill.name: pr-review
skill.description: Review pull requests for the MiniMax Skills repository. Use when reviewing PRs, validating new skill submissions, or checking existing skills for compliance. Run the validation script first for hard checks, then apply quality guidelines for content review. Triggers: PR review, pull request, validate skill, check skill.
skill.summary: Review pull requests for the MiniMax Skills repository. Use when reviewing PRs, validating new skill...
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: All 70 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner misinterpreted Markdown backtick code fences (used for documentation formatting) as Ruby/shell command execution. Blocker findings for 'weak cryptographic algorithm' matched on secret-scanning regex patterns in the validation script, not actual cryptography. 'System reconnaissance' findings matched standard PR review terminology in documentation. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, or unsafe behavior was found. The skill is a legitimate PR review tool with a Python validation script that uses only standard library modules.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives - Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 41 instances of Markdown backtick code fences as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are documentation formatting characters, not command execution. All findings in SKILL.md, references/quality-guidelines.md, and references/structure-rules.md are inline code examples or code blocks showing users how to run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations are within Markdown code fences or inline backtick spans. These files contain no executable Ruby or shell code.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives - Secret Scanning Regex Misidentified
low_findings.description: 26 blocker findings for 'weak cryptographic algorithm' matched on regex patterns used for secret detection (sk-, AKIA, Bearer token patterns) in scripts/validate_skills.py and documentation describing what the scanner looks for. These are not cryptographic operations but string pattern matching for credential detection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The patterns are regex strings for detecting API keys, not cryptographic algorithm calls. Confirmed by reading scripts/validate_skills.py lines 76-80.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives - Documentation Terminology
low_findings.description: Low-severity 'system reconnaissance' findings matched on standard PR review terminology such as 'review', 'check', and 'scan' in documentation text. These are not system scanning operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Matched terms appear in prose documentation describing review processes, not in executable code performing reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Review pull requests for skill quality
content.value_statement: Manual review of pull requests in the MiniMax Skills repository can miss structural issues and quality standards. This skill provides automated validation and guided content review to ensure every skill meets repository standards before merging.
actual_capabilities: Run automated validation of skill directory structure and YAML frontmatter
Check for required fields in SKILL.md frontmatter
Scan files for hardcoded API keys and secrets
Apply quality guidelines during manual content review
Verify naming conventions and directory structure compliance
Validate README synchronization for new skill additions
limitations: Only validates skills in the MiniMax Skills repository structure
Secret scanning detects only high-confidence patterns (OpenAI keys, AWS keys, Bearer tokens)
Content review guidelines are advisory and require human judgment
Does not validate the functional correctness of skill scripts

use_cases.title: Automated PR validation for skill submissions
use_cases.description: Run the validation script to check new skill submissions against structural requirements before manual review begins.

use_cases.title: Quality review of existing skills
use_cases.description: Apply content review guidelines to evaluate skill descriptions, file sizes, and credential handling practices.

use_cases.title: Standardizing skill documentation
use_cases.description: Ensure all skills follow consistent naming, frontmatter format, and documentation standards across the repository.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PR review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request for the MiniMax Skills repository. Run the validation script first, then check the PR against the quality guidelines.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick review of a single skill submission

prompt_templates.title: Validate a new skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate the new skill in this PR. Check that SKILL.md has valid frontmatter, required fields, no hardcoded secrets, and follows the kebab-case naming convention.
prompt_templates.scenario: Reviewing a first-time contributor skill submission

prompt_templates.title: Full quality audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a complete review of this PR. Phase 1: run validate_skills.py and check all ERROR items pass. Phase 2: review description quality, file size, API key handling, script quality, language, and README sync status.
prompt_templates.scenario: Thorough review of a complex skill with scripts

prompt_templates.title: Audit multiple skills
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the validation script across all skills in the repository. List any that fail ERROR checks or have WARNING items. For failures, summarize what needs to be fixed.
prompt_templates.scenario: Periodic health check of the entire skills repository

output_examples.input: Review PR #42 adding a new weather-api skill
output: Phase 1 Validation: PASS - validate_skills.py exited with code 0
- Skill scope: No overlap with existing skills
- Description quality: Includes trigger conditions and keywords
- File size: SKILL.md is 45 lines, references are 120 lines total
- Credential handling: API key documented as required environment variable
- Script quality: Shebang present, requirements.txt included, error handling clear
- README sync: Both README.md and README_zh.md updated with Community source
Name matches directory. Frontmatter valid. No secrets detected.

best_practices: Always run validate_skills.py before starting manual content review
Check that API credentials are documented as environment variables, not hardcoded values
Verify the README.md and README_zh.md tables are updated when adding new skills
anti_patterns: Approving a PR without running the validation script first
Ignoring WARNING items that indicate missing license or metadata fields
Allowing hardcoded API keys or passwords in skill files or scripts

faq.question: What does the validation script check?
faq.answer: The script checks for SKILL.md presence, valid YAML frontmatter, required fields like name and description, directory name matching the frontmatter name, and scans for hardcoded secrets.

faq.question: Can this skill review skills outside the MiniMax repository?
faq.answer: No. The validation script and guidelines are specific to the MiniMax Skills repository structure and conventions.

faq.question: What happens if the validation script fails?
faq.answer: ERROR-level failures must be fixed before the PR can merge. The script exits with code 1 when errors are found.

faq.question: Are WARNING items blockers?
faq.answer: No. WARNING items such as missing license or metadata fields should be flagged but do not block merging.

faq.question: What secret patterns does the scanner detect?
faq.answer: It detects OpenAI-style API keys (sk- prefix), AWS Access Key IDs (AKIA prefix), and hardcoded Bearer tokens (JWT format). Other credential patterns require manual review.

faq.question: Does this skill require external dependencies?
faq.answer: No. The validation script uses only Python standard library modules. No pip install is required.
